primarySetting: The whole story takes place in ONE cozy child's bedroom at night: the child's bed, a chair with a shirt hung on its back (whose shadow climbs the wall), and the bedroom window with night outside. Uri the fox appears on the windowsill with his neck lantern. It is the SAME night bedroom on every page — never a clinic, never daylight, never another room, never outdoors beyond the night seen through the window.
forbiddenDrift: an actual monster (the scary shape is only the chair and shirt)
more than one Uri / duplicate or cloned fox
redesigning the bedroom set between pages
transitionRules: Stay in the SAME night bedroom on every page — camera may move, the room must not redesign.
Never cut to a clinic, a daytime room, another room, or step outdoors; night is only seen through the window.
The chair-shadow and the window branch are the same objects throughout; only what the lantern reveals about them changes.

scenes.label: The night bedroom
scenes.description: One cozy child's bedroom at night — the child's bed, a chair with a shirt on its back casting a shadow on the wall, and the window with night outside. The single set for the whole book.
visualAnchors: the chair with a shirt on its back

recurringObjects.label: the child's bed
recurringObjects.identity: The SAME child's bed every page with the same blanket. A child's bed, never a crib or hospital bed.

stateTimeline.state: the child lies in bed, blanket pulled up to the nose, watching the wall
the child rests back on the pillow in the same bed, calm now

recurringObjects.label: the chair with the shirt
recurringObjects.identity: The SAME chair near the bed with a shirt hung over its back; its tall shadow climbs the wall. It is ONLY a chair and a shirt — never a real creature. Same chair and same shirt every page.
forbiddenDrift: the shirt becoming a different garment

stateTimeline.state: the tall chair-shadow on the wall looks almost alive, like a coat-monster
the lantern light reaches it and reveals only part of the shadow
fully revealed as just the chair and the hanging shirt — one sleeve looked like a long neck, one button like a sleepy eye
still casts a chair-shape shadow on the wall, but no longer scary

recurringObjects.label: the bedroom window
recurringObjects.identity: The SAME bedroom window with night outside; a small branch outside taps the glass. Same window every page; Uri perches on its sill.
forbiddenDrift: a different window shape or position
an open door or skylight instead of the window

stateTimeline.state: Uri the fox first appears on the windowsill, lifting his neck lantern
the child listens toward the window, recognizing the sound comes from there
a little branch outside is revealed tapping the glass — the source of the tapping sound
Uri sits on the windowsill then slips outside, only a grounded tail-tip hint remaining near the window edge

forbiddenDrift: any clinic, hospital, or medical setting
daylight or a bright daytime room
any room other than this one night bedroom
an actual monster instead of the chair-and-shirt shadow
more than one fox or child
